model.description: Alibaba • A balance between effectiveness, speed, and cost.

pick.rationale: Gonzaga enters as a substantial favorite despite the absence of Braden Huff (17.8 PPG), who has been sidelined since January with a knee injury. The Bulldogs remain ranked #11 nationally with an elite defense (9th in Torvik) that should contain Kennesaw State's offense, while star forward Graham Ike is healthy and leading the team. Kennesaw State won the CUSA Tournament and boasts a potent scoring offense (31st nationally at 83.4 PPG), but their poor defense (239th in points allowed) and mediocre overall profile (151st SRS) create a significant vulnerability against a power-conference opponent. Historical 3/14 matchup data heavily favors Gonzaga, and Mark Few's extensive tournament experience (nine Sweet 16 appearances since 2014) provides a decisive edge in this Round of 64 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga vs Kennesaw State - 3/14 West Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Gonzaga favored despite Huff injury due to elite defense, tournament experience, and substantial quality gap over Kennesaw State
reasoningStep.evidence: Gonzaga ranked #11 in KenPom/Torvik with 30-3 record and 9th-ranked defense (adjde 93.97); Braden Huff (17.8 PPG) out since January 15 with knee injury but Graham Ike healthy; Kennesaw State 21-13, won CUSA Tournament, 31st in scoring (83.4 PPG) but 239th in defense (76.1 PA/G), 151st SRS; 3-seeds beat 14-seeds approximately 85% historically; Gonzaga has 9 Sweet 16 appearances since 2014 under Mark Few
